interactions.label: Camera Type
interactions.teaches: Changing this option demonstrates how different intrinsic parameters affect the projection of points into the image plane.

interactions.label: Rotation (R)
interactions.teaches: Adjusting the rotation changes how the world coordinates are transformed into camera coordinates.

interactions.label: Translation (T)
interactions.teaches: Modifying the translation shifts the camera's position relative to the world coordinates.

demo_steps.title: Default Camera Model
demo_steps.narration: We start with the standard camera model. Notice the transformation of point P from world coordinates to camera coordinates and finally to the image plane.

demo_steps.title: Rotate Camera
demo_steps.narration: Let's rotate the camera. As you increase the rotation slider, observe how point P's location changes in the camera coordinates, affecting the projection.

demo_steps.title: Translate Camera
demo_steps.narration: Next, we move the camera along its translation vector. Changing this parameter shifts each point proportionally in both camera and image coordinates.

demo_steps.title: Switch Camera Type
demo_steps.narration: Finally, explore different camera types. Switching from 'Standard' to 'Orthographic' changes the nature of projection from perspective to parallel, affecting how 3D points map to the image plane.

interactionPlan.camera_suggestion: View from the side, slightly above, to clearly see all coordinate transformations and their effects on point P.
interactionPlan.notes: Utilize Three.js features for matrices and 3D transformations to simulate realistic camera behavior.
